channel.title: Morning Mail
channel.description: Stay updated on the latest news and events happening in the RPI community.

item.title: Undergraduate Research Symposium
item.description: Date: Friday, April 17, 2026 1:00 PM - 3:00 PM - The 2026 Undergraduate Research Symposium will be held at 1:00 PM on Friday, April 17 in the CBIS Atrium. The event, which is free and open to everyone, will feature presentations from undergraduate RPI students who are currently or have previously worked in URP. Any students interested in presenting a poster are encouraged to reach out to their URP coordinator, applications are open now!
item.author: Students

item.title: CAS SciFinder Upcoming Webinars
item.description: RPI Libraries invites you to attend upcoming vendor-hosted CAS SciFinder webinars that include topics like Agentic AI in CAS SciFinder, How to search structures and retrieve references in CAS IP Finder™, and CAS Formulations in CAS IP Finder™ Explore on-demand webinars, including: What’s New in CAS SciFinder®?, 2026 Trends to Watch: Expert Panel, and Data Mining and Analysis: Unlocking New Research Perspectives with AI See the full announcement for registration information.
item.author: Students

item.title: Mario Kart & Pizza Break with Lally
item.description: Take a break from classes and join students from across campus for an afternoon of Mario Kart, pizza, popcorn, and Lally swag in PITTS 5200. Whether you’re racing to win or just here for the pizza, it’s the perfect way to recharge and unwind as the semester wraps up. Wednesday, April 15 | 11:30 a.m.–2:00 p.m. No sign-up needed, just drop in and play!
item.author: Students

item.title: From Model-Optimal to Human-Optimal: Human-Centered Optimization for the Common Purposes of Life
item.description: Please join ISE on Wednesday, April 22 at 12:15 PM in LOW 3039 for a colloquium featuring Dr. Andrew Trapp’s research on From Model-Optimal to Human-Optimal: Human-Centered Optimization for the Common Purposes of Life! All are welcome!
item.author: Community

item.title: The Design Showcase Welcomes Guest Speaker, Brent Brunell ’94 from GE Vernova
item.description: Brent Brunell, ’94, the Technology Director of Digital & Electrical Systems at GE Vernova Advanced Research, will visit campus as a featured guest speaker for the Design Lab Showcase on Wednesday, April 22, 2026. Brent’s keynote presentation beginning at 3:00 PM in DCC 324.
item.author: Community

item.title: Design Lab Showcase to be held Wednesday, April 22nd, 2026 from 1:00 - 4:00 p.m.
item.description: The Design Showcase will be held on Wednesday, Wednesday, April 22nd, 2026 from 1:00 - 4:00 p.m. in the DCC Great Hall. Students from multiple engineering disciplines (Computer & Systems, Electrical, Industrial & Management, Materials and Mechanical Engineering) solve real-world design challenges for industry, entrepreneurs and academic/medical partners. Visit the showcase to: Interact with project teams and view their achievements. Learn how students solve complex design challenges. Get…
item.author: Community

item.title: Summer Internship Opportunity in the Design Lab!
item.description: Project: Capstone Team Formation and Project Assignment Tool Work description: This project will develop an AI-powered software tool to automate and optimize the student-to-project assignment process. The intern will build ML/NLP-powered tools to extract student competencies from unstructured data, map them to project requirements, and automatically generate optimized student-project matches based on interests and experience — all supported by the Design Lab team. For more information: https:/…
item.author: Community

item.title: RPI Concert Choir and Wind Symphony Spring Concert
item.description: Join the RPI Concert Choir and the RPI Wind Symphony for their Spring Concert on Thursday, April 16th, at 7pm in the Chapel and Cultural Center at 2125 Burdett Avenue on RPI Campus. Admission is free and open to the whole RPI community.
item.author: Community

item.title: Webinar Friday April 17: Webex Event Training
item.description: Hosting a campus event or supporting a student webinar? Join a hands-on Webex Event Training Webinar, part of the MMS Collaboration Software Training series. Register now for this webinar presented via Webex on Friday April 17 from 11am-12pm: https://rensselaer.webex.com/webappng/sites/rensselaer/webinar/webinarSeries/register/783d951c6ab8482e9198a7bd6075a700 Learn the basics of Webex Event hosting, with demonstration and Q&A, along with new features recently added to the hosting platform.
item.author: Community

item.title: QTPiE Emerging Scholar Grant – Call for Proposals
item.description: Queer and Trans People in Education (QTPiE) invites proposals from emerging scholars interested in queer and trans education research. This grant supports collaborative quantitative research that advances equity for queer and trans people in education. Proposals are due Friday, 5/1. Visit https://www.qtpiecollective.org for the full Call for Proposals and details. Questions? Email jcgarvey@uvm.edu
item.author: Community

item.title: Join us at EMPAC for Corpus Festival — Opening day & Community Meal! (RSVP)
item.description: Date: Thursday, April 23, 2026 (all day) - EMPAC presents a weekend of creative experiments and gatherings that attune us to how bodily forms and bodies in formation construct our world. Bring your curiosity and your appetite: How do we take part in society by physically showing up? RSVP in advance for the community meal — a free, family-style, long-table supper co-hosted by artist Yanira Castro, with food by Chef Ria Ibrahim, musical offerings by Taína Asili — and more. Installations open…
item.author: Community

item.title: Computer Science Thesis Defense - Bolun (Namir) Xia
item.description: Date: Friday, April 17, 2026 9:00 AM - 11:00 AM - Dissertation defense: Using Semantically Enhanced Machine Learning Models for Prediction and Querying on Financial Documents
item.author: Community

item.title: Division of Human Resources Professional Development Workshop: “Getting Started with Percipio: Navigation & Q&A”
item.description: Date: Friday, April 17, 2026 9:00 AM - 9:30 AM - This brief course is designed to help you get comfortable using Percipio, RPI’s digital learning platform. You’ll learn how to navigate the main dashboard, search for content, access assigned learning, and track your progress. We’ll also walk through common questions users have and show you where to find help when needed. Whether you’re brand new or just need a refresher, this session will ensure you’re ready to make the most of Percipio.
item.author: Faculty and Staff

item.title: 2026 Faculty and Staff Climate Survey Launched
item.description:
The 2026 Faculty and Staff Climate Survey launched by email on April 2, 2026. Your honest and candid opinions about the environment at RPI and how it affects your ability to do you work and thrive, professionally and personally, are important. You will find a personal link to complete the survey in your email from President Schmidt. Nearly 50% of employees completed this survey in 2024 and so far, we have approximately 22% completed. Please submit the completely anonymous survey by April 30,…

For information about sharing your news or events, or to change your subscription settings, visit morningmail.rpi.edu.
item.author: Faculty and Staff
